title: Country Code Field for Mobile Number in the End User's Form Submission View
excerpt:
The Country Code field for Mobile Number column, in the End User’s Form Submission view, provides unfavorable user experiences and challenging to operate for average users (Boomers.) 
We need to request a user’s accurate…

posts.author: cwiassoc (Andrew Beckwith)
posts.cookedHtml:
The Country Code field for Mobile Number column, in the End User’s Form Submission view, provides unfavorable user experiences and challenging to operate for average users (Boomers.)

We need to request a user’s accurate mobile number, so they must complete this field on a shareable form link. All of our requestees are located in the United States. The (+1) American Flag is buried at the bottom of the dropdown in alphabetical order (even though it shows the flags and not the country name.)

I suggest any of the following:

  1. Make the United States the default area code
  2. Make the Account Owners Country of Residence the default area code
  3. Move the United States to the top of the drop-down list

posts.author: cfraticelli (Celene Fraticelli)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree with this request, but I’d like the ability to completely remove the country code of the phone number. Could that be a setting within the column?

posts.author: cwiassoc (Andrew Beckwith)
posts.cookedHtml: This is fantastic. Ability to remove it completely please. Thank you!!!

posts.author: Knowa (Noah)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please… The first complaint when I demo’d using a form for our office staff was that entering phone numbers is a PITA. 100% of our business is within 1 state of the US, having to type out united states every single form is really unfriendly user experience. I would absolutely kill for the ability to set a default country or remove the country code entirely.

posts.author: Bria-CSSOS (Brian Robertson)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a solution for this yet? I have the same issue and would love to either be able to set the top values or get rid of it completely.

posts.author: cwiassoc (Andrew Beckwith)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t believe so. Still using the same flag based system as before.

posts.author: Bria-CSSOS (Brian Robertson)
posts.cookedHtml: Ok thats what I thought. Damn wish they would have jumped on this sooner to fix. That is the worse dropdown.

posts.author: bhowell (Bryan Howell)
posts.cookedHtml: I believe I’ve requested this in the past. It’s just a cumbersome function for our use as our customers are all located within our state, nonetheless our country! I can understand monday having this function as they seem to be based across the globe, but our company is not, so it’s a feature that should be able to be filtered or hone in a bit more in my opinion.

posts.author: HTOHagan (Thomas OHagan)
posts.cookedHtml: I am in the same boat Clients can’t figure out how to use the drop-down for the Country I even wrote in the form. Please type united states, and they don’t like it. I went back to the text column for the phone number, and the formatting is never consistent.

posts.author: bhowell (Bryan Howell)
posts.cookedHtml:
@monday are there any plans to make some adjustments to this? Going back and having to add a “1” before phone numbers so that they don’t look crazy from the format that our team is used to is a bit of an unnecessarily tedious task.

Thank you!

posts.author: jlaroche (Jean LaRoche)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just the possibility to program a default would suffice.

Once in a blue moon, someone from another country-code could simply pull down / select another code. In the column property at the top, you (Monday) could fix a way to set a default value.

posts.author: bhowell (Bryan Howell)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone figured out if perhaps an automation could add a “1” before every phone number entered? This is an ongoing limitation frustration of mine. None of our employees are used to typing a “1” in front and it seems like such an annoyance to have to keep nagging them to do so when it’s not something that is every required in any other use case of our business or life in general. Monday, can you hear me? Please at least consider some sort of option or setting that addresses this!

posts.author: Elliot (Elliot Cryer)
posts.cookedHtml:
US appears as the top option now. That will suit most users.

BUT I am in AUS and want my country on-top haha.

Actually I would prefer the option for a default as many have asked.
The option for removing all together could be nice but may have formatting issues so the default option would be best.

posts.author: Leon (Leon)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree that Country code should be optional. Need the ability to remove it from form. This is redundancy for us as all our business is in the USA.
Hope someone from Monday.com will read this topic.

posts.author: Alan (Alan Crumbley)
posts.cookedHtml: I am in AUS as well and would prefer it to be removed or default to the relevant country.

posts.author: Eillen (Eillen Sobremonte)
posts.cookedHtml: Still no updates on this? I also prefer to have an option to set the default country code.

posts.author: Mackadelic (Mack)
posts.cookedHtml:
@monday-team @monday-moderators

Still no movement on this request, this is extremely difficult for organizing my CRM boards! Is there any work around yet or anything at all that can be done?

posts.author: edan (Edan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any word on this?

I’m facing an issue when using Make and being required to add a +1 to phone numbers.

People that live in the US wouldn’t type their number like that, which makes everything a lot more challenging.

Technically, to make the phone number field work, I’ll need to try and detect what county the phone number is and perfect the number so that it can get added properly.

This experience feels off putting.

posts.author: Vtab (Vero Tabares)
posts.cookedHtml: Would love to see this change. We have to add a text field on any board that needs a phone number, but without an input mask option, people enter numbers in every format imaginable. 99.9% of our business is done in the US.

posts.author: Kaitlynw2113 (Kaitlyn Rose)
posts.cookedHtml: SAAMMEE. @monday-team @CXS @CSG Please help with this request! People have been asking for this update for many years. To be specific, please make the country code in the phone column optional, or allow a default! This +1 issue wreaks havoc on my automations and integrations. I was previously just using a text type column for phone numbers but now I have other automations (those used to send calls and texts from within monday.com) that require phone numbers to be listed in the phone number type column. It creates such a headache to have to reformat every number when they’re all in the US!

posts.author: huntermcqueen (Hunter O McQueen)
posts.cookedHtml: This should be a simple but MUCH needed fix! Having to manually add a 1 in front over every number defeats the purpose of automating the retrieval.

posts.author: Operations (Dawn Heart)
posts.cookedHtml: By simply clicking on the phone number, our company is able to use Zoom or Google Voice to call clients. I have inherited hundreds of clients, who do not have “1” in front of the phone #, so it registers as an international call. Going back & adding “1” in front of every number is a bear. Is there a way to create a formula column to put the “1” in?

posts.author: jlaroche (Jean LaRoche)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi. I just saw this was fixed and quite well, in fact. In the form, there are now two ways to preformat the country code. Perfect. At last.

posts.author: RussK (Russ K)
posts.cookedHtml: A welcome addition to the form option! Just wish we could do this in the table/column view also.

posts.author: fureveraftermemorial (Michelle)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any update on this? Ideally we’d like to be able to turn off the country code completely. Our clients are ONLY in our country, and the automatic formatting to include the country code messes with our integrations.

posts.author: mondayuser6 (Jim)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding on this pile-on. @monday, have you used the phone field on a mobile device? The one we just tested on has the country field wider than the phone field. The user had no idea what to do. they kept clicking the country field wondering where to enter the phone number. I guess we’ll put in a text field for the phone and hope for the best.
